summary: Tested THE BHARAT news app admin panel + home feed end-to-end. Backend: 15/15 pytest cases PASS against /api/admin/login, /api/admin/news, /api/admin/ads, /api/news, /api/ads, /api/states, /api/languages — including create→GET persistence verification and admin-news-top-ordering. Frontend (Expo web, 375x667 viewport): all 7 review-request flows are functional. Admin login with 'sunil@123' works and shows success banner; both 'Create News' and 'Create Ad' submissions succeed (verified via backend logs showing 200 OK for POST /api/admin/news and POST /api/admin/ads); image upload via web file input fallback works; forms clear after submit; EXCLUSIVE badge renders on admin news; ADVERTISEMENT cards render in the feed; coin modal, state selector, language selector all open and select correctly without overflow.

minor.issue: No authentication required after /api/admin/login — endpoint can be called without any token/session. Login is purely client-side state.
Same — no auth header / session check on admin endpoints.
Returns 200 instead of 201 on resource creation (consistency).

When state=='Maharashtra' (or non-National/International), filter only matches news with that exact state. Admin news created with state=National will NOT appear when user selects Maharashtra — by design but worth confirming intent.

ui_bugs.issue: After creating admin news in admin panel and returning via back-button, the home feed does NOT auto-refresh — the new EXCLUSIVE news only appears after pull-to-refresh or after changing state/language. useEffect only depends on [selectedLanguage, selectedState].
Status banner auto-dismisses after 4s — fine, but on slower connections users may miss the success confirmation. Consider keeping success banner until next action.

issues: State/Language selector buttons use flex:1 and may truncate long state names (e.g., 'Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu') — numberOfLines={1} handles overflow, but full name is hidden. Acceptable but worth noting.

react_native_issues.issue: Deprecation warning: 'SafeAreaView has been deprecated... use react-native-safe-area-context'. Non-blocking but should be migrated.
Many 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' warnings flooding console. Non-blocking on web but noisy.
Warning: 'Image: style.resizeMode is deprecated. Please use props.resizeMode' — currently set inside StyleSheet.

action_items: Add a focus/refetch trigger on Home so it re-fetches news+ads when the screen regains focus (e.g., useFocusEffect from expo-router) — this fixes the reported 'cannot see uploaded news' UX gap.
(Optional security) Protect /api/admin/* endpoints with a token returned by /api/admin/login instead of relying on client-side state.
Move resizeMode from StyleSheet into the Image component props to silence deprecation warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: admin endpoints lack auth — anyone with the URL can POST news/ads. Fine for MVP, risk-flag for production.
index.tsx: news+ads fetch only re-runs on language/state change — no pull-on-focus, leading to user-reported 'I can't see my upload' confusion.
admin.tsx: image upload uses base64 data URLs sent inline in JSON — large images may exceed FastAPI default body size and slow Mongo writes. Consider multipart upload + object storage for production.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed news/ads and clean up via DELETE in teardown. UI test also created 'UI TEST Admin News Title' news and 'UI TEST Ad Title' ad which remain in DB (admin can delete via API if needed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend admin APIs fully functional and tested via /app/backend/tests/test_admin_apis.py. Run with: pytest backend/tests/test_admin_apis.py -v. Frontend admin flow works end-to-end on Expo web preview. The only real UX issue is home feed not auto-refreshing after admin upload — fix by using useFocusEffect on home screen.
rca_of_issue: User report 'cannot upload news/ads through admin panel' was NOT a backend or upload failure — backend POSTs return 200 OK and data persists in MongoDB (verified). Root cause is likely user perception: (1) the cross-platform success banner now works on web (window.alert + on-screen banner) and was confirmed visible during test; (2) admin-created news DID appear in the API response (verified by GET /api/news showing it at index 0 with isAdminNews:true and EXCLUSIVE badge in UI). The remaining user-visible gap: after navigating back from admin to home, the home feed doesn't refetch automatically — so a user who just uploaded news and tapped back may think nothing happened. Fix: add useFocusEffect on home to refetch news/ads when screen gains focus.
